meta.description: Species information for Paradise Seafood product pages. Each entry provides About, Why Chefs Love It, and Health & Nutrition content for SEO and user value.
meta.usage: Map products to species data using the subcategory/image_key field. Display on subcategory pages and individual product pages.

cod.name: Cod
cod.about: A cornerstone of British cuisine with firm white flesh and a mild, clean flavour. Takes on seasoning beautifully. Works across every cooking method — battered for fish and chips, roasted with herbs, pan-fried for fine dining, or flaked into chowders and fish pies.
chefs: Consistent portion sizes from 110g to 900g+ means easy menu costing
MSC certified options available for sustainability-conscious menus
Loins and fillets hold their shape under heat — no flaking apart mid-service
Year-round availability means it's a reliable menu staple
health: Virtually zero fat — one of the leanest fish available
Excellent source of vitamin B12 for energy and nerve function
Rich in selenium which supports thyroid function and immune health
High in iodine and phosphorus for bone health

salmon.name: Salmon
salmon.about: The most versatile fish in any kitchen. Scottish salmon is prized for its rich flavour and buttery texture. Equally at home raw as sashimi, cured as gravadlax, pan-seared skin-side down, or hot-smoked for brunch service. Loch Duart and organic options for premium menus.
chefs: Available in every cut — whole, fillet, supreme, loin, steak
Supremes come portion-ready so there's zero prep waste
The colour on the plate is unmatched — that deep orange-pink sells itself
Skin crisps up perfectly for that textural contrast diners love
health: One of the richest sources of omega-3 fatty acids — supports heart health and reduces inflammation
High in vitamin D which most people are deficient in
Contains astaxanthin, a powerful antioxidant that gives it the pink colour
Rich in B vitamins including B12, B6, and niacin

sea-bass.name: Sea Bass
sea-bass.about: Elegant and refined with delicate white flesh and subtle sweetness. A fine dining staple that pairs beautifully with Mediterranean and Asian flavours. Available farmed (consistent supply, competitive price) and wild (superior flavour, premium price point).
chefs: Skin scores and crisps beautifully — the most photogenic fish on any plate
Farmed bass available in 6 size grades means you pick the exact portion for your menu
Wild bass commands a premium price point that lifts your average spend
Fillets are virtually boneless — minimal prep time
health: Low calorie at just 97 calories per 100g
Good source of omega-3s for heart and brain health
Rich in selenium which supports immune function
Contains magnesium for muscle and nerve function

halibut.name: Halibut
halibut.about: The king of flatfish. Dense, meaty flesh that holds up to any cooking method — roasting, pan-frying, steaming, even braising. Highly prized for its clean flavour and satisfying, almost steak-like texture. Available wild and farmed, whole or as supremes.
chefs: Thick dense flesh means it won't overcook easily — forgiving during busy service
Supremes come in 100g to 200g portions for precise menu costing
White, clean presentation on the plate
One of the highest-margin fish on any fine dining menu
health: One of the highest protein fish at 22g per 100g
Excellent source of niacin (vitamin B3) which supports metabolism
Rich in phosphorus for strong bones and teeth
Contains selenium and magnesium for immune and muscle health

monkfish.name: Monkfish
monkfish.about: Known as "poor man's lobster" for its firm, sweet, lobster-like flesh. The tail is the prized cut — meaty, boneless, and incredibly versatile. Holds up brilliantly to roasting whole, wrapping in pancetta or Parma ham, grilling, or cutting into medallions.
chefs: Zero pin bones — the easiest fish to prep in any kitchen
Holds its shape on the grill and in stews where other fish would fall apart
The texture is unique — firm and meaty, satisfying even for diners who "don't like fish"
Tails from 300g to 4kg+ for any menu format from tapas to sharing plates
health: Extremely lean — under 1g of fat per 100g
High protein at 18g per 100g with minimal calories
Good source of vitamin B12 and phosphorus
One of the best fish choices for low-fat and high-protein diets

tuna.name: Tuna
tuna.about: From sashimi-grade loins to seared steaks, tuna is one of the most prized fish in global cuisine. The deep ruby-red flesh and rich, almost meaty flavour set it apart. Best served rare or raw — overcooking is the only way to ruin it. Toro (belly) is the fattiest, most luxurious cut.
chefs: Sushi-grade tuna commands premium prices with minimal prep — slice and plate
The colour on the plate is dramatic and Instagram-worthy
A 30-second sear per side is all it needs — fastest premium dish in any kitchen
Versatile across Japanese, Mediterranean, and modern European menus
health: Exceptionally high protein — 25g per 100g, one of the highest of any fish
Rich in vitamin D and selenium for immune and bone health
Contains iron which is unusual for fish — good for energy levels
Omega-3s support brain function and cardiovascular health

haddock.name: Haddock
haddock.about: A British classic with softer, slightly sweeter flesh than cod. The backbone of the smoked fish world — natural smoked haddock is one of the most beloved breakfast and brunch ingredients in the UK. Fresh haddock works beautifully battered, poached, or in fish pies.
chefs: MSC certified options available across all size grades
Smoked haddock is a breakfast and brunch menu essential — kedgeree, Arnold Bennett omelette, chowder
Lighter texture than cod gives variety to fish-heavy menus
Competitive price point with strong customer demand
health: Very low fat at under 1g per 100g
Excellent source of vitamin B12 — a single portion provides over 100% of daily needs
Rich in selenium for thyroid and immune support
High in iodine which supports thyroid health

hake.name: Hake
hake.about: A favourite across Spanish, Portuguese, and increasingly British kitchens. Hake has delicate, flaky white flesh with a slightly sweeter flavour than cod. Supremes are the preferred cut — belly-trimmed and portion-ready. Often overlooked but gaining serious traction on fine dining menus.
chefs: MSC certified whole hake available — strong sustainability story
Supremes come belly-trimmed and portion-ready, minimal waste
Lighter and more delicate than cod — ideal for spring and summer menus
Popular across Mediterranean cuisine — pairs with olive oil, peppers, chorizo
health: Very low in fat and calories — one of the lightest white fish
Good source of vitamin B12 and B6 for energy
Rich in phosphorus for bone health
Contains selenium and iodine for thyroid function

turbot.name: Turbot
turbot.about: The most prized flatfish in European cuisine. Turbot has firm, snow-white flesh with a rich, almost nutty flavour that chefs adore. Available wild-caught from UK waters and farmed from Spain. Traditionally roasted on the bone for maximum flavour.
chefs: The prestige fish — having turbot on your menu signals serious fine dining
Firm flesh holds up to roasting on the bone, the classic French presentation
Wild turbot commands top prices and justifies a premium menu position
Available from under 1kg to 5kg+ — suitable for individual portions or sharing dishes
health: Good source of lean protein with moderate fat content
Rich in omega-3 fatty acids compared to other white fish
Excellent source of vitamin B12 and B6
Contains phosphorus, selenium, and magnesium

dover-sole.name: Dover Sole
dover-sole.about: The classic fish of fine French cuisine. Dover sole has firm, dense flesh with a distinctive, refined flavour that improves with a day or two of resting. Traditionally pan-fried whole in butter (à la meunière) or served on the bone for tableside filleting.
chefs: The ultimate tableside theatre — served whole and filleted in front of the guest
Flesh actually improves 1-2 days after catch — rare quality for fish
Six size grades from 320g to 670g+ for precise menu positioning
Simple butter, lemon, and capers is all it needs — the fish does the talking
health: Low in fat and calories while being high in protein
Good source of vitamin B12 for nerve health
Contains selenium and phosphorus
Mild flavour makes it ideal for guests who find stronger fish off-putting

sea-bream.name: Sea Bream
sea-bream.about: Gilt-head sea bream is one of the most popular fish in Mediterranean cuisine. Beautiful silver and gold scales, delicate white flesh, and a mild sweet flavour. Available farmed (consistent quality and price) and wild black bream (deeper flavour, UK waters).
chefs: One of the most beautiful whole fish to present — the gold markings are stunning
Farmed bream comes in multiple size grades from 300g to 1kg for precise portioning
Crisps beautifully when pan-fried or grilled — similar to sea bass but at a better price point
Wild black bream offers a premium upgrade for seasonal specials
health: Good source of lean protein with moderate healthy fats
Contains omega-3 fatty acids for heart health
Rich in selenium and vitamin B12
Low calorie option at 100 calories per 100g

john-dory.name: John Dory
john-dory.about: One of the great fish for fine dining. John Dory has firm, white, delicately flavoured flesh that's often compared to Dover sole in quality. Recognisable by the distinctive dark spot on each side — legend says it's Saint Peter's thumbprint. Low yield but exceptional eating.
chefs: Exceptional flesh quality rivals Dover sole at a lower price point
The story of Saint Peter's thumbprint is a menu narrative that guests love
Firm fillets that hold up to pan-frying and don't break apart
Available in four size grades from under 350g to 908g+
health: Very lean white fish with minimal fat
Good source of protein and vitamin B12
Contains selenium for immune support
Low calorie at just 85 per 100g

plaice.name: Plaice
plaice.about: A beloved British flatfish with thin, delicate flesh and a mild sweet flavour. Recognisable by the orange spots on its dark brown skin. Best pan-fried, grilled, or baked — its gentle flavour pairs with butter, capers, and herbs. A traditional favourite that's making a comeback on modern menus.
chefs: The orange-spotted skin is distinctive and attractive on the plate
Delicate flavour that works as a lighter alternative to cod or haddock
Available whole and as fillets in multiple size grades
Competitive price point makes it a strong value option for set menus
health: Very low in fat and calories
Good source of vitamin B12 and selenium
Contains iodine for thyroid function
Gentle on the stomach — easy to digest

red-mullet.name: Red Mullet
red-mullet.about: A jewel of Mediterranean cuisine with vibrant red-pink scales and a distinctive, slightly earthy flavour. Prized across French, Italian, and Greek cooking. Best cooked whole — the liver is considered a delicacy. Works beautifully pan-fried, grilled, or baked en papillote.
chefs: Stunning visual impact — the red colour is dramatic on any plate
The liver is a delicacy in French cuisine — adds depth to sauces
Small fish means quick cooking — ideal for starters and sharing plates
Available whole in four size grades and as butterfly fillets
health: Good source of omega-3 fatty acids for a small fish
Rich in iron compared to most white fish
Contains vitamin A and selenium
High in protein relative to its size

red-snapper.name: Red Snapper
red-snapper.about: A tropical reef fish prized for its firm, moist, mildly sweet flesh. The vivid red skin is stunning and edible when crisped. Popular across Caribbean, Asian, and Latin American cuisines. Works beautifully whole — steamed, grilled, or baked with bold flavours.
chefs: The bright red skin is one of the most visually striking presentations in seafood
Firm flesh holds up to bold flavours — chilli, ginger, lime, coconut
Available whole from 300g to 6kg — from individual portions to dramatic sharing plates
Fillets are thick and meaty with clean white flesh
health: High in protein with very low fat content
Good source of selenium and vitamin A
Contains potassium for heart health
Rich in omega-3 fatty acids

mackerel.name: Mackerel
mackerel.about: One of the most nutritious fish in the sea and one of the most beautiful — iridescent blue-green tiger stripes that shimmer. An oily fish with rich, bold flavour that stands up to smoking, grilling, and strong accompaniments like mustard, horseradish, and beetroot. Incredibly sustainable and British.
chefs: One of the most sustainable fish — fast-growing, abundant in British waters
The bold flavour means it pairs with strong accompaniments that would overpower delicate fish
Line-caught mackerel is a powerful provenance story for your menu
V-boned fillets available for quick prep — 80g per fillet, ideal for starters
health: One of the highest omega-3 fish — over 2.5g per 100g
Excellent source of vitamin D — critical for bone health
Rich in vitamin B12, B6, and niacin for energy
Contains selenium and iodine for thyroid function

sardines.name: Sardines
sardines.about: Small, silvery, and packed with flavour. Fresh sardines are a world apart from the tinned version. Best grilled whole over charcoal, barbecued, or baked with garlic and herbs. A staple of Portuguese and Mediterranean cuisine. MSC certified options available.
chefs: Incredibly affordable — high-impact dish at a low food cost
MSC certified options for sustainability-focused menus
Grill or barbecue whole in minutes — minimal prep, maximum flavour
Butterfly fillets available for more refined presentations
health: Extremely rich in omega-3 fatty acids
One of the few fish where you eat the bones — excellent calcium source
High in vitamin D and B12
Contains CoQ10, an antioxidant that supports heart health

skate.name: Skate
skate.about: Skate wings have a unique texture — long strands of flesh that pull away from the cartilage. The flavour is sweet and mild, slightly nutty. Classically served with brown butter, capers, and lemon (skate au beurre noir). Available double-skinned and ready for the pan.
chefs: A classic French bistro dish that never goes out of style
The unique texture gives variety to any fish menu
Available in four size grades from under 350g to 908g+ wings
Double-skinned wings are ready to cook — no further prep needed
health: Low in fat and calories with good protein content
The cartilage structure means it's rich in collagen
Contains vitamin B12 and selenium
Good source of phosphorus for bone health

trout.name: Trout
trout.about: From wild brown trout to farmed rainbow, trout offers a lighter, more delicate alternative to salmon. Beautiful speckled skin with a pink lateral stripe. Works well pan-fried, baked, or hot-smoked. Sea-reared trout bridges the gap between salmon and trout — rich flavour with trout's elegance.
chefs: Sea-reared trout offers salmon-like richness at a lower price point
Beautiful whole fish presentation — the speckled skin is stunning
Rainbow trout supremes available in three sizes for precise portioning
Wild brown trout is a seasonal delicacy that commands a premium
health: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids — higher than most white fish
Excellent source of vitamin D and B12
Contains potassium and phosphorus for heart and bone health
Lower mercury levels than larger predatory fish

swordfish.name: Swordfish
swordfish.about: A bold, meaty fish with dense, steak-like flesh. Swordfish loins are cut into thick steaks that hold up to grilling, chargrilling, and barbecuing. The flavour is rich and slightly sweet. Best served medium — still slightly pink in the centre.
chefs: The most "steak-like" fish — appeals to meat lovers and fish sceptics alike
Thick loins and steaks hold up to the grill without falling apart
Supremes available in three sizes for consistent portioning
A summer menu hero — perfect for outdoor dining and barbecue events
health: High in protein with moderate healthy fat content
Excellent source of selenium — one portion provides full daily needs
Rich in vitamin D and niacin (B3)
Contains zinc and potassium for immune and heart health

hamachi.name: Hamachi (Yellowtail)
hamachi.about: A Japanese premium fish prized for sashimi and sushi. Hamachi has buttery, pink-cream flesh with beautiful fat marbling — often compared to wagyu beef in the fish world. The fat content gives it a rich, melt-in-the-mouth quality that's unmistakable.
chefs: The sashimi fish — hamachi crudo is one of the most popular starters in modern fine dining
Fat marbling means it literally melts in the mouth — diners remember this fish
Works raw, seared, or as tataki — versatile across Japanese and fusion menus
Whole fish and fillets available for different volume needs
health: High in protein with healthy monounsaturated fats
Rich in omega-3 fatty acids and EPA/DHA
Excellent source of vitamin D and B vitamins
Contains selenium, iron, and niacin

lobster.name: Lobster
lobster.about: The ultimate luxury shellfish. Sweet, succulent meat from the claws and tail. Native British lobster has a darker shell and richer, more complex flavour than imported Canadian. Available live in five sizes from 450g to 900g+, plus cooked and as tails.
chefs: Nothing says "special occasion" like lobster — it lifts your entire menu's perceived value
Live lobster guarantees freshness and gives theatre to open kitchens
Native vs imported gives you two price points for different menu positions
The shell makes a stock that elevates every sauce in your kitchen
health: High protein, low fat, low calorie at just 89 per 100g
Extremely rich in zinc — supports immune health and wound healing
Excellent source of vitamin B12 and copper
Contains EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids for heart health

langoustine.name: Langoustine
langoustine.about: Also known as Dublin Bay prawns or Norway lobster, langoustines are one of Britain's most prized shellfish. Delicate, sweet flesh in slender claws and tails. Scottish langoustines are world-renowned — sweeter and more refined than any prawn. Best served simply to let the natural flavour shine.
chefs: Scottish langoustines are considered some of the best shellfish in the world
The sweetness and delicacy surpasses any prawn — a genuine luxury ingredient
Available live for ultimate freshness and raw dipped for convenience
Tails, claws, and shells all have value — zero waste if you make bisque from the shells
health: Very low in fat and calories with good protein content
Rich in zinc and selenium for immune support
Contains vitamin B12 and copper
Good source of phosphorus for bone health

scallop.name: Scallop
scallop.about: Sweet, buttery, and delicate — the jewel of shellfish. King scallops seared for 90 seconds per side is one of the simplest and most impressive dishes a chef can serve. Diver-caught are premium — hand-collected with minimal seabed impact, less grit, better flavour.
chefs: The ultimate "less is more" ingredient — three scallops and a purée makes a £16 starter
Sear time is under 3 minutes — fastest premium starter during service
Diver-caught is a genuine sustainability story for your menu
The coral (roe) adds colour and makes a beautiful sauce
health: One of the lowest calorie shellfish at just 69 calories per 100g
Excellent source of vitamin B12 — one of the richest shellfish sources
High in magnesium and potassium for heart health
Rich in zinc and selenium for immune support

oyster.name: Oyster
oyster.about: Eaten raw with lemon, Tabasco, or mignonette — oysters are pure terroir. You can taste the sea they came from. Mersea rocks, Maldon, Fines de Claire, and natives each have distinct character. The ultimate bar snack and the classiest way to start any meal.
chefs: Highest margin item on any seafood menu — minimal prep, maximum price
An oyster bar or raw bar elevates your restaurant's positioning instantly
Seasonal natives give you a rotating menu talking point
They sell drinks — oysters and champagne is the most profitable pairing in hospitality
health: The single richest food source of zinc — six oysters provide 5x your daily needs
Extremely high in vitamin B12, iron, and copper
Low calorie at just 68 per 100g
Contains unique antioxidants found only in shellfish

crab.name: Crab
crab.about: British brown crab is one of the great ingredients of our coastline. Sweet white meat from the claws and legs, rich brown meat from the body. Available whole live, cooked, dressed, and as picked meat. Devon crab meat is the gold standard. Spider crab and velvet crab also available.
chefs: White and brown meat give you two distinct ingredients from one animal
Dressed crab is a classic starter that never goes out of fashion
Picked meat saves hours of prep — Devon white meat is the cleanest available
Crab claws make an impressive visual centrepiece for sharing platters
health: High in protein with very low fat and calories
Excellent source of zinc and copper for immune function
Rich in vitamin B12 and selenium
Contains omega-3 fatty acids and chromium

prawns.name: Prawns
prawns.about: The workhorse of professional kitchens worldwide. From fresh raw tiger prawns to frozen king prawns in every format — shell-on, peeled and deveined, head-on, cooked, block, and IQF. Sizes from U5 giants to 41/50 cocktail prawns. Used in everything from Thai curries to prawn cocktails to paella.
chefs: Every size and format means one supplier covers all your prawn needs
Frozen IQF means zero waste — defrost what you need, keep the rest
Fresh tiger prawns for premium presentations, frozen for volume dishes
The most universally liked protein across every cuisine and every diner
health: Very high in protein at 24g per 100g with minimal fat
Rich in selenium — one portion provides over 50% of daily needs
Good source of zinc and vitamin B12
Contains astaxanthin antioxidant which gives prawns their colour

king-prawns.name: King Prawns
king-prawns.about: The frozen king prawn is the backbone of commercial kitchens. Available in every conceivable format — fresh water and sea water, block and IQF, headless shell-on, peeled and deveined, head-on shell-on, cooked and raw. Sizes from U5 (giants) to 41/50 (small). Origins spanning Asia, Bangladesh, Mediterranean, and Argentina.
chefs: The most versatile frozen protein — works in any cuisine from Asian to European
IQF format means individual prawns defrost in minutes for quick service
Block format offers better value for high-volume dishes like curries and paella
Consistent sizing means uniform cooking and plating every time
health: Very high in protein with virtually no fat
Rich in selenium and zinc for immune health
Good source of vitamin B12 and iodine
Low calorie and low carbohydrate

squid.name: Squid
squid.about: From crispy calamari to chargrilled whole squid, this is one of the most versatile cephalopods in the kitchen. Available as clean tubes, whole baby squid, and chipirones. The key rule: cook it for 30 seconds or 30 minutes — anything in between and it's rubber.
chefs: Calamari is one of the highest-margin starters on any menu
Baby squid (chipirones) grill beautifully whole for Mediterranean sharing plates
Tubes come cleaned and ready to stuff, slice into rings, or score and sear
Available frozen year-round — no seasonal gaps
health: High in protein with low fat content
Rich in vitamin B12 — one of the highest among shellfish
Good source of copper, selenium, and zinc
Contains riboflavin (B2) for energy metabolism

octopus.name: Octopus
octopus.about: Once a niche ingredient, octopus is now a fine dining staple. Spanish and Mediterranean origins. The secret to tender octopus is slow braising — then finishing on a hot grill or plancha for char. Available whole in block format and as baby octopus for different applications.
chefs: Chargrilled octopus is one of the most Instagram-worthy dishes in modern cuisine
Slow-braised then grilled gives the perfect tender-crisp contrast
Baby octopus works beautifully in salads, stews, and as tapas
Can be prepped in advance — braise on Monday, grill to order all week
health: Low calorie and low fat with good protein
Extremely rich in vitamin B12 — one of the highest food sources
High in iron compared to most seafood
Contains selenium, copper, and zinc

smoked-salmon.name: Smoked Salmon
smoked-salmon.about: One of Britain's great artisan products. Traditional cold-smoking over oak produces a silky texture and complex flavour that elevates any plate. Available long-sliced, D-cut, as whole sides, and in organic, oak-smoked, and Severn varieties. Kiln-roast (hot smoked) offers a flakier, more rustic alternative.
chefs: A breakfast, brunch, starter, and canapé essential — used across every day-part
Long-sliced sides give you maximum coverage with minimal waste
Organic and oak-smoked options for premium menu positioning
Shelf life is significantly longer than fresh fish — reduces waste
health: Retains most of fresh salmon's omega-3 benefits through smoking
High in protein at 18g per 100g
Rich in vitamin D — one of the few food sources
Note: higher sodium than fresh due to curing — serve in appropriate portions

smoked-haddock.name: Smoked Haddock
smoked-haddock.about: A British institution. Natural undyed smoked haddock has a pale golden colour and delicate smoky flavour. The backbone of classic dishes — kedgeree, Arnold Bennett omelette, cullen skink, and fish pie. MSC certified options available. Yellow-dyed is available but natural is the premium choice.
chefs: Essential for brunch menus — kedgeree and Arnold Bennett omelette are classics that sell
MSC certified options available for sustainability messaging
Natural undyed is the premium choice — yellow dyed is available for traditional fish and chip shops
Poaches beautifully in milk — the cooking liquid becomes the base for your sauce
health: High protein with very low fat
Excellent source of vitamin B12 and selenium
Rich in iodine for thyroid health
Note: contains higher sodium than fresh haddock due to smoking process

caviar.name: Caviar
caviar.about: The pinnacle of luxury. Beluga, Oscietra, and Sevruga each offer a different experience — from creamy and buttery to briny and intense. Served chilled on a mother-of-pearl spoon, traditionally with blinis, crème fraîche, and champagne. Available in 20g to 50g tins.
chefs: Nothing else on your menu carries the same cachet — caviar is the ultimate luxury signal
Small tins (20g-30g) make it accessible as a garnish rather than a standalone course
Three varieties (Beluga, Oscietra, Sevruga) give you a range of price points
Pairs with champagne — the most profitable combination in hospitality
health: Extremely nutrient-dense — rich in omega-3s, B12, vitamin A, and selenium
High in iron at 12mg per 100g
Contains vitamin D and choline which supports brain function
One of nature's most concentrated sources of essential fatty acids

mussels.name: Mussels
mussels.about: One of the most sustainable and affordable shellfish. Rope-grown mussels are farmed with minimal environmental impact. Classic moules marinières (mussels in white wine, garlic, and cream) is one of the great dishes of French cuisine. Available rope-grown and wild, in 2kg and 5kg bags.
chefs: One of the best food-cost ratios in seafood — high impact, low cost
Moules frites is a menu staple that customers order repeatedly
Rope-grown is a strong sustainability story — low environmental impact
Cook from raw in under 5 minutes — ideal for quick service
health: Rich in iron — one of the best shellfish sources
Excellent source of vitamin B12 and selenium
High in manganese and zinc for immune health
Sustainable protein source with very low carbon footprint

clams.name: Clams
clams.about: From delicate palourdes to dramatic razor clams, clams add elegance to any seafood menu. Vongole (clams with spaghetti) is one of Italy's great pasta dishes. Available in five varieties — amandes, palourdes, razor (medium and small), and Venus surf clams.
chefs: Spaghetti alle vongole is one of the simplest and most beloved pasta dishes
Razor clams are visually dramatic — grilled with garlic butter is a showstopper
Multiple varieties give you options from rustic to refined
Quick to cook — most clams open in 3-4 minutes
health: Extremely high in vitamin B12 — one of the richest food sources
Rich in iron, especially for shellfish
Good source of selenium and manganese
Low calorie and low fat

soft-shell-crab.name: Soft Shell Crab
soft-shell-crab.about: Whole crabs harvested just after moulting, when the new shell is still soft and entirely edible. Deep-fried, the entire crab becomes crispy and delicious. A favourite in Japanese tempura, Vietnamese banh mi, and American po'boys. Available frozen in four sizes from Thailand.
chefs: Deep-fry from frozen in 3 minutes — one of the quickest proteins to serve
The entire crab is edible — no waste, no shell cracking, no fuss
Visually dramatic on the plate — a whole crab you eat in one go
Four sizes available from whales (9s) to hotels (18s) for different menu positions
health: Good source of protein with low fat before frying
Rich in zinc and copper for immune function
Contains vitamin B12 and selenium
High in calcium due to eating the entire shell

king-crab.name: King Crab
king-crab.about: The most impressive shellfish you can put on a table. Massive leg clusters from Norway and Russia, with sweet, snow-white meat inside dramatic red-orange shells. Available raw and cooked. A centrepiece ingredient that turns any meal into an event.
chefs: The most dramatic presentation in all of seafood — the sheer size impresses every guest
Sweet, delicate meat that needs almost nothing — melted butter and lemon
Available raw (Norway) and cooked (Russia) for different kitchen setups
A single cluster feeds two people as a starter or one as a main — easy portioning
health: High protein with very low fat and calories
Extremely rich in zinc — supports immune function
Excellent source of vitamin B12 and copper
Contains omega-3 fatty acids and selenium

black-cod.name: Black Cod (Sablefish)
black-cod.about: Made famous by Nobu's miso black cod, this is one of the most luxurious fish in modern fine dining. Silky, buttery flesh with a high fat content that melts in the mouth. MSC certified from USA/Canada. Available frozen headed and gutted.
chefs: Nobu's miso black cod put this fish on the global fine dining map — instant menu recognition
The high fat content means it's almost impossible to overcook — incredibly forgiving
MSC certified — a premium fish with a genuine sustainability story
Marinates beautifully — miso, sake, soy, and brown sugar all work perfectly
health: Exceptionally high in omega-3 fatty acids — one of the richest fish sources
The high fat content is almost entirely healthy unsaturated fats
Rich in vitamin A, D, and E
Contains selenium and phosphorus

chilean-sea-bass.name: Chilean Sea Bass (Patagonian Toothfish)
chilean-sea-bass.about: One of the most premium frozen fish in the world. Thick, snow-white fillets with a rich, buttery flavour and large, moist flakes. MSC certified from the Falkland Islands. Despite the name, it's not actually a bass — it's a deep-water species from the Southern Ocean.
chefs: One of the highest-value fish on any menu — commands premium prices
MSC certified from the Falkland Islands — a powerful sustainability and provenance story
The thick fillets are incredibly forgiving — stays moist even if slightly overcooked
Works with bold Asian flavours (miso, ginger) and classic European preparations equally well
health: Very high in omega-3 fatty acids — comparable to salmon
Rich in vitamin D and vitamin A
Contains selenium and phosphorus for immune and bone health
The high fat content provides sustained energy

samphire.name: Samphire
samphire.about: The quintessential seafood garnish. Samphire grows wild along the British coastline and has a crisp, salty, sea-spray flavour that pairs perfectly with any fish dish. Also known as sea asparagus. Available farmed (year-round) and wild (seasonal, more intense flavour).
chefs: The ultimate finishing touch for any fish plate — adds colour, texture, and seasoning in one
Wild samphire is seasonal — a talking point for your menu that signals ingredient knowledge
Quick blanch in unsalted water (it's naturally salty) is all it needs
Works raw in salads or wilted as a warm garnish
health: Extremely low calorie at just 22 per 100g
Rich in vitamins A and C for immune health
Contains iodine, iron, and calcium
High fibre content for digestive health

anchovy.name: Anchovy
anchovy.about: Far more than a pizza topping. Quality anchovies — white anchovies in oil, garlic anchovies, smoked anchovies — are a foundation ingredient in Mediterranean cuisine. They add umami depth to dressings, sauces, and butters. Fresh whole anchovies are available for grilling and frying.
chefs: The secret weapon of professional kitchens — add umami depth without anyone knowing it's there
Seven varieties available from white in oil to smoked to salted — each has a different application
Essential for Caesar dressing, bagna cauda, puttanesca, and tapenade
Fresh whole anchovies deep-fried are a spectacular bar snack
health: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids for heart and brain health
Extremely high in calcium — one of the best sources among fish
Contains iron and selenium
High in niacin and vitamin B12 for energy

roe.name: Roe & Tobiko
roe.about: The jewels of Japanese cuisine. Tobiko (flying fish roe) comes in orange, green (wasabi), and yellow varieties. Masago (capelin roe) is smaller and more affordable. Used as garnish for sushi, poke bowls, canapés, and as a finishing touch on any seafood dish.
chefs: Instant visual luxury — a spoonful of tobiko transforms any dish
Three colours (orange, green, yellow) for different visual effects
Masago is a more affordable alternative that works in high-volume applications
Long shelf life frozen — defrost what you need, keep the rest
health: Good source of protein with low fat
Rich in omega-3 fatty acids
Contains vitamin B12 and selenium
High in phosphorus and riboflavin

lemon-sole.name: Lemon Sole
lemon-sole.about: A delicate, sweet-flavoured flatfish that's more affordable than Dover sole but still delivers elegance on the plate. Smooth, light-brown skin and thin, tender fillets. Best pan-fried in butter or baked simply. A classic British fish that's been served in restaurants for generations.
chefs: Delivers a Dover sole-like experience at a significantly lower price point
Whole fish presentation is elegant — works for both fine dining and brasserie menus
Quick to cook — thin fillets need just 2-3 minutes per side
Available whole in two size grades and as frozen fillets year-round
health: Very low in fat and calories
Good source of vitamin B12 and selenium
Contains phosphorus for bone health
Light and easy to digest

stone-bass.name: Stone Bass (Meagre)
stone-bass.about: An impressive large fish that's gaining popularity in UK fine dining. Firm, white flesh with a clean, mild flavour similar to sea bass but with a meatier texture. Can grow very large — available from 1kg to 2kg+. Also known as meagre or corvina.
chefs: Meaty texture holds up to grilling, roasting, and bold flavours better than sea bass
Supremes available in 170/190g portions for consistent plating
Less well-known means it sparks curiosity on a menu — a talking point
Large fish means high fillet yield and less waste per kg
health: High protein with low fat content
Good source of omega-3 fatty acids
Contains selenium and vitamin B12
Low calorie at under 100 per 100g

mahi-mahi.name: Mahi Mahi
mahi-mahi.about: A tropical fish with firm, slightly sweet flesh and stunning golden-green iridescent skin. Also known as dolphinfish (no relation to dolphins). Popular across Caribbean, Hawaiian, and modern fusion cuisines. Best grilled, pan-seared, or blackened with bold spice rubs.
chefs: Firm flesh that holds up perfectly on the grill — ideal for summer menus
The iridescent skin is one of the most beautiful in all of seafood
Works with bold, punchy flavours — jerk spice, mango salsa, coconut curry
Available as skinless boneless portions for consistent plating
health: Very lean with high protein — excellent for health-conscious diners
Good source of vitamin B12 and selenium
Contains potassium and niacin
Low mercury levels compared to other large tropical fish
